body.u: I have led a pretty normal life. I left school when I was nearly eighteen and got a job. The money was quite good, but carried on living with my parents. I kept that job for eight months and then got a job as a personal secretary because the money was much better. When I was nineteen, I got married to my boyfriend who I’d known at school. It was good at school because you met so many people and had so many friends. We used to go out alota lot to school discos and have a good time. When I left school, I missed it even though I made new friends at work, but everything became more serious.

Two years after I got married, I gave up working to have my first child. As soon as the child was old enough to go to school, I started working again- not because we were poor but it doesn’t hurt to have a bit of extra money, and I got bored being stuck at home all day. With both my husband and myself working, we came fairly well off. This meant I could have lots of new clothes and could afford to go out more often. We still saved alota lot of money though.

At the age of twenty-eight I had my second child, and so, had to give up working again, but this didn’t last for long. As soon as my second child went to school, I went back to work. I gave my children a good upbringing so that I could be proud of them. One of my children gained some A levels, whilst the other one got some o levels and left school, but they both got good jobs.
My father died when I was forty although I knew he’d die sooner or later, I was still shocked and upset. Five years later, my mother died and I inherited a fairly large sum of money.

Now I’m eighty- I don’t know how I’ve managed to live this long. My children have left and got married and have also made me a grandmother three times. But I’m alone now. My husband has gone. When he died, I thought I’d die without him, but I lived.
Now, all I have to look forward to, is my funeral and being buried next to my husband.
